intro.title: Graphs generated by the Tool
intro.description: Real-time visualizations from CoreLab's statistical engine. Charts below are placeholders — they will refresh from the most recent analysis run on the Tool page.

sections.title: Research design at a glance
sections.lead: A descriptive-correlational design comparing Preliminary and Midterm grading periods across 145 IT students at STI College Malolos.

sections.title: How the data was collected
sections.left: A 4-part survey: lab attendance hours, hands-on activity ratio, perceived performance shift, and Prelim → Midterm grade record.
right: Likert scale items validated through Cronbach's α
Numerical inputs cross-checked against the registrar's records
Pilot-tested with a non-respondent cohort prior to deployment

items.label: Lab hours × performance change
items.text: Moderate-to-strong positive correlation between weekly attended laboratory hours and the change in grade between Prelim and Midterm.

items.label: Multiple regression model
items.text: Lab hours combined with hands-on ratio explain about 47% of the variance in academic improvement.

items.label: Survey reliability
items.text: Cronbach's α confirms the perception items behave as a coherent measure of one underlying construct.

sections.title: Reading the results
sections.quote: Students who spent more weekly hours in the computer laboratory — particularly when those hours were dominated by hands-on activity — showed measurable academic improvement between the Preliminary and Midterm grading periods.
sections.footnote: Causality is not established; lab usage is one of several plausible drivers of the observed change.

steps.title: H₀ rejected
steps.text: Evidence supports a positive association between lab usage and performance change.

steps.title: Hands-on time matters
steps.text: Time spent actively using lab tools is a stronger predictor than passive presence.

steps.title: Curriculum implication
steps.text: Increase guided hands-on hours in IT major subjects and track effects across grading periods.

steps.title: Future work
steps.text: Extend to multi-semester data and incorporate program type as a moderator.
